proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, median 65 - clinically consistent with patient age in years) is positively correlated with pfs_months (i.e., higher feature_078 is associated with longer pfs_months).

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.856 between feature_078 and pfs_months (p effectively 0). Mean pfs_months rises monotonically across deciles of feature_078: 0.01 mo (30-36) -> 7.82 mo (84-90). feature_078 is the dominant univariate predictor of pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal, values 0/1/2 - clinically consistent with disease stage or extent) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_057 implies shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.pearsonr(df['feature_057'], df['pfs_months']); df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.388 (p ~ 0). Mean pfs_months by level: 4.68 mo (level 0, n=17592), 3.49 mo (level 1, n=24971), 2.38 mo (level 2, n=7437). Strong, monotone, negative relationship - consistent with stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (continuous, right-skewed, median 15.86, max ~3622 - clinically consistent with serum PSA in ng/mL) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code:
import numpy as np
df['log_psa'] = np.log1p(df['feature_013'])
stats.pearsonr(df['log_psa'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r between feature_013 and pfs_months on raw scale = -0.153 (p=3.5e-260). On log1p scale the relationship is approximately linear; in a multivariable OLS adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, feature_006 and feature_009, log(1+feature_013) coefficient = -0.383 mo per log unit (p~0). Higher feature_013 robustly predicts shorter pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, ~25% zeros, median 3.0 - clinically consistent with a tumor-burden or symptom score) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_006'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.143 (p=9.9e-226). In the multivariable model the adjusted slope is -0.0768 mo per unit increase in feature_006 (p~0). Robust negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, median 3.8 - clinically consistent with a hemoglobin / albumin-style protective lab) is positively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_009'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.123 (p=1.5e-166). Adjusted slope = +0.479 mo per unit (p~0). Higher feature_009 is associated with longer pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (ordinal, range 6-10 - clinically consistent with Gleason sum) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_067 implies shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_067'], df['pfs_months']); df.groupby('feature_067')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.003 (p=0.486). Mean pfs_months across feature_067 levels 6/7/8/9/10 is essentially flat (3.71/3.76/3.74/3.74/3.70). H6 NOT supported on the univariate scale; in the adjusted model levels 8/9/10 are about 0.02 mo lower than 6 (p<0.05) - statistically detectable but clinically negligible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence 0.55) is associated with shorter pfs_months on the univariate scale.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 4.03 (feature_051=0, n=22485) vs 3.51 (feature_051=1, n=27515); difference = -0.52 mo, Welch t-test p = 1.6e-181. Strong univariate negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for log(1+feature_013) (PSA-like) and the other clinical covariates, the residual main effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is essentially zero - i.e., feature_051's univariate signal is mediated by feature_013.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_psa + feature_006 + feature_009 + C(feature_067)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_051 = +0.0007 mo (p=0.86). Confirmed mechanism: mean log(1+feature_013) = 2.17 in feature_051=0 vs 3.55 in feature_051=1 (t-test p~0). feature_051 is essentially a marker of high feature_013, and its raw negative association with pfs_months is fully explained by elevated feature_013 in those patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across racial groups encoded in feature_018 (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_018']==r,'pfs_months'] for r in df['feature_018'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA F = 0.564, p = 0.689. Mean pfs_months: white 3.741, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, asian 3.797, other 3.749. The largest pairwise gap (asian vs hispanic) is 0.06 mo. H9 NOT supported - no detectable racial difference in raw pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance categories encoded in feature_045 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured), with uninsured patients having the shortest pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_045']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df['feature_045'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA F = 0.344, p = 0.794. Mean pfs_months: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. Uninsured group has only a 0.028 mo lower mean than medicare; not significant. H10 NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical features (feature_078, feature_057, feature_067, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_051), at least one race level in feature_018 retains a non-zero effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + C(feature_067) + log_psa + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All four feature_018 contrasts vs reference are tiny and non-significant: black +0.0020 (p=0.80), hispanic +0.0015 (p=0.85), other -0.0010 (p=0.94), white +0.0028 (p=0.68). Largest |effect| ~ 0.003 mo. H11 NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical features, at least one insurance level in feature_045 retains a non-zero effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: Same multivariable OLS as h11; inspect feature_045 contrasts.
analyses.result_summary: All three feature_045 contrasts are tiny and non-significant: medicare +0.00039 (p=0.94), private +0.00035 (p=0.94), uninsured +0.00122 (p=0.89). H12 NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model containing feature_078, feature_057, feature_067, log(1+feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, and feature_051 explains the large majority of variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.9).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + C(feature_067) + log_psa + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051', data=df).fit().rsquared
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.9692. Just 7 features (one continuous age, one ordinal stage, one ordinal Gleason, log-PSA, two more continuous labs, and the binary feature_051) account for ~97% of the variance in pfs_months. H13 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the highest-stage subgroup (feature_057 = 2, advanced disease, n=7437), pfs_months differs across racial groups in feature_018 - i.e., disparities are concentrated where prognosis is worst.

analyses.code: sub = df[df['feature_057']==2]; stats.f_oneway(*[sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==r,'pfs_months'] for r in sub['feature_018'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA F = 0.895, p = 0.466. No racial heterogeneity in pfs_months even within advanced-stage patients. H14 NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, prevalence 0.10) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_109']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_109']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 3.734 (feature_109=0, n=45004) vs 3.834 (feature_109=1, n=4996); difference = +0.100 mo, p = 2.05e-3. Modest but statistically clear positive univariate association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 (binary, prevalence 0.10) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_039']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_039']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 3.735 (feature_039=0, n=44902) vs 3.829 (feature_039=1, n=5098); difference = +0.094 mo, p = 3.3e-3. Similar magnitude and direction to feature_109.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 act SYNERGISTICALLY: patients with both feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1 have substantially longer pfs_months than expected from the additive contributions of each marker alone (positive interaction term).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109*feature_039 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + C(feature_067) + log_psa + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Cell means (raw): (109=0, 039=0) 3.74 mo; (0,1) 3.67; (1,0) 3.67; (1,1) 5.22. Adjusted main effects of feature_109 and feature_039 alone are ~0 (p>0.85 each). The interaction term feature_109*feature_039 = +1.434 mo (95% CI 1.40-1.47, p effectively 0). Adding the interaction raises model R^2 from 0.969 to 0.974. Strong synergy: the marker-pair contributes ~1.4 extra months of PFS only when both are positive (~1% of the cohort, n=970).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariate negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by feature_057 (stage): the magnitude of the gap is largest in advanced-stage (feature_057=2) patients.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests within feature_057 strata; OLS with feature_051*C(feature_057).
analyses.result_summary: Stratified mean PFS difference (feature_051=1 vs 0): stage 0 -0.559 mo (p=7.3e-84), stage 1 -0.514 (p=5.2e-105), stage 2 -0.472 (p=8.9e-31). Magnitude is similar (and slightly LARGER in stage 0, opposite to the hypothesis). In the adjusted interaction model the feature_051*stage1 coef = +0.017 (p=0.018) and *stage2 = +0.061 (p<1e-9), but main effect ~0 - i.e., very small attenuation of the (already-mediated) feature_051 effect at higher stage. H18 NOT supported as stated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic combination feature_109*feature_039 produces a similar magnitude of pfs_months benefit across racial subgroups (no race x combo interaction beyond sampling noise).

analyses.code: Within-race t-tests of pfs_months for combo (109=1 & 039=1) vs not.
analyses.result_summary: Within-race combo benefit: asian +1.44 mo (n=30, p=0.038), black +1.75 (n=58, p=2.5e-5), hispanic +0.99 (n=78, p=7.9e-3), white +1.53 (n=355, p=1.2e-19), other +2.81 (n=8, p=0.053). All point estimates positive and within the 1.0-2.8 mo range; the formal interaction term in OLS is statistically non-zero only for the small 'other' bucket. Magnitude of benefit is comparable across the major racial groups - H19 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic combination feature_109*feature_039 produces a similar magnitude of pfs_months benefit across insurance subgroups in feature_045.

analyses.code: Within-insurance t-tests of pfs_months for combo vs not.
analyses.result_summary: Within-insurance combo benefit: medicare +1.41 mo (n_combo=224, p=8.4e-11), private +1.52 (n=203, p=3.4e-11), medicaid +1.78 (n=78, p=1.2e-6), uninsured (n_combo too small for stable estimate). All major groups show benefit of 1.4-1.8 mo - similar magnitude. H20 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Prevalence of the predictive markers feature_109 and feature_039 (and their combination) is balanced across race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) - i.e., no demographic disparity in marker positivity.

analyses.code: Chi-square tests of feature_109 and feature_039 vs feature_018 and feature_045.
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 prevalence by race (asian 10.7%, black 9.4%, hispanic 10.0%, other 9.7%, white 10.0%) chi^2=3.80, df=4, p=0.434. feature_039 by race chi^2=5.26, p=0.261. By insurance: feature_109 chi^2=2.95, p=0.399; feature_039 chi^2=1.73, p=0.63. Combo prevalence 0.8-1.1% across all race / insurance categories. No demographic disparity in marker prevalence. H21 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (univariate) negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months varies by race: at least one race x feature_051 interaction is non-zero.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_018) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_psa + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All four feature_051 x feature_018 interaction terms (vs asian reference) are not significant: black +0.023 (p=0.14), hispanic -0.006 (p=0.70), other +0.0003 (p=0.99), white +0.010 (p=0.48). H22 NOT supported - no racial heterogeneity in feature_051's effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months varies by insurance: at least one feature_045 x feature_051 interaction is non-zero.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_045) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_psa + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All three feature_051 x feature_045 interactions non-significant: medicare -0.011 (p=0.27), private -0.002 (p=0.83), uninsured +0.004 (p=0.84). H23 NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (Gleason-like) modifies the effect of feature_051 on pfs_months: at least one feature_067 x feature_051 interaction is non-zero.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_067) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_psa + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All four feature_051 x feature_067 interactions (vs Gleason 6 reference) non-significant: G7 +0.003 (p=0.79), G8 +0.009 (p=0.44), G9 +0.011 (p=0.41), G10 +0.016 (p=0.35). H24 NOT supported - feature_067 does not modulate the (already null after PSA-adjustment) feature_051 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_018 (race) and feature_045 (insurance) to the parsimonious clinical model {feature_078, feature_057, feature_067, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_051, feature_109*feature_039} does NOT improve fit (joint F-test p > 0.05): outcome variation in this cohort is fully captured by clinical / biomarker variables.

analyses.code: from statsmodels.stats.anova import anova_lm; m_base = smf.ols(clinical_formula, data=df).fit(); m_full = smf.ols(clinical_formula + ' + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit(); anova_lm(m_base, m_full)
analyses.result_summary: Nested-model F-test, base R^2 = 0.974451 vs full R^2 = 0.974451 (ssr drop 0.031 over 7 added df): F = 0.042, p = 0.99991. Including race and insurance changes adjusted variance explained by < 1e-5. Outcome (pfs_months) is fully determined by clinical / biomarker variables in this synthetic cohort with NO demographic confounding or disparity. H25 supported.
